steps.observed: Test PASS. Logs 'error: test expected 1 values, found 2'. Function continues with actual num_vals.
Test PASS. Nibbles correctly extracted with high nibble first, low nibble second, and padding verified as 0.

All 8 tests PASS. Sub-tests include: n=0_bit2=1 (increment=0), n=1_bit2=1 (increment=1), n=1_bit2=1_bit3=1 (increment=-1), n=0_bit2=0 (zero-length read), multi-byte increment, negative increment, single byte increment, three byte increment (with expected error log for 3-byte check), negative three byte increment, negative single byte increment, negative zero increment.

assertions.title: decode_stroke_values — delta decoding

steps.expected: Test passes with Python's actual delta algorithm producing [5, 8, 10, 12, 16]
steps.observed: Test PASS. Delta decoding produces [5, 8, 10, 12, 16] for increments [5, 3, -1, 0, 2], matching Python's actual algorithm (i==0: change=0, value=increment; else: change+=increment, value+=change).

assertions.issues: Known discrepancy: Validation contract VAL-D-015 claims delta decoding produces [5,13,20,27,36] for increments [5,3,-1,0,2], but this is incorrect. Python's actual algorithm produces [5,8,10,12,16]. Our Go implementation correctly follows Python (source of truth). Test verifies Python's actual values.

steps.expected: Test passes (exit code 0), returns empty slice
steps.observed: Test PASS. Empty input returns empty values with no error.

steps.expected: Test passes (exit code 0), extra data error logged
steps.observed: Test PASS. Logs 'error: test_extra has extra data: ff' when trailing bytes present after decoding all points.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: all 7 passed (VAL-D-011 through VAL-D-017). Known discrepancy confirmed for VAL-D-015: validation contract claims delta decoding produces [5,13,20,27,36] but Python's actual algorithm produces [5,8,10,12,16]. Go implementation correctly follows Python (source of truth).
